title: Spec-Driven Development Deep Dive — Part 2: GitHub Spec Kit Practical Guide
description: Get to know the GitHub Spec Kit: a practical toolkit for implementing Spec-Driven Development (SDD) through 9 core instructions, helping you eliminate specification drift in AI coding.
content:
# Spec-Driven Development Deep Dive — Part 2: GitHub Spec Kit Practical Guide

**GitHub Spec Kit** is an open-source toolkit released by GitHub, designed to provide 9 standardized commands (such as...)`/speckit.specify` and `/speckit.plan`Enforcing Specification-Driven Development (SDD) is crucial. By constraining the behavior of the Large Language Model (LLM), it ensures that code implementation is always aligned with business intent, fundamentally eliminating "spec drift."

**Series Navigation:**

- [Part 1: The Evolution of Software Design and the Essence of SDD](/posts/sdd-series-part-1-evolution/)

- **Part 2 (This Post): A Practical Guide to GitHub Spec Kit**

- [Part 3: Intent Layer Infrastructure and the Future of SDD](/posts/sdd-series-part-3-future/)

- [Part 4: Building Simple SDD Workflows Using GitHub Issues](/posts/sdd-series-part-4-github-issues/)

![Minimalist Illustration of GitHub Spec Kit](/assets/img/post/sdd-series/sdd-spec-kit-minimalist.png)

*Illustration: GitHub Spec Kit — Encapsulating Intent Specifications into a Highly Systematized Command-Line Infrastructure*

## 1. What is a Spec Kit?

GitHub Spec Kit is a Spec-Driven Development (SDD) toolkit open-sourced by GitHub in September 2025. It doesn't replace AI coding assistants, but rather is a suite for managing constraint component architectures (along with scaffolding and structured templates), supporting integration with core tools like GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, and Cursor for specification convergence.

### Core Concept: The Only Source of Truth Amidst Change

In traditional workflows, once business development is complete, documentation immediately becomes rusty (i.e., specification drift). Teams tend to treat code as an absolute, isolated source of truth.

However, with Spec Kit, **specifications are directly equivalent to code build recipes**. When you change your mind, you only need to modify the specification document, and AI directly recompiles and modifies the output code according to the blueprint, instead of forcing developers to painfully modify mountains of code. This concept is similar to the idea of ​​breaking down tool silos using the powerful [Model Context Protocol (MCP)](/posts/mcp-apps-guide/). **Primary Suitable Scenarios:**

- Greenfield projects starting from scratch

- Modern iterative engineering with highly customized constraints for complex intentions

- Large, complex ecosystems requiring rigid multi-user collaboration and avoiding cross-terminal system deviations

| Terms | Definitions |

| :--- | :--- |

| **Spec Kit** | An open-source SDD toolkit on GitHub, containing a set of Slash Commands and standard templates. |

| **Spec Drift** | Specification drift. Inconsistencies between code implementation and business documentation that arise over time. |

| **Artifacts** | Artifacts. Refers to those generated during the lifecycle.`spec.md`、`plan.md`、`tasks.md`Based on facts. |

## 2. GitHub Spec Kit Core Architecture: 5 Cores + 4 Extensions

In the design of the **GitHub Spec Kit**, instructions are rigorously divided into **Essential Commands** and **Optional Commands** for enhancing quality/coordination. Understanding this layering is a prerequisite for efficient SDD application.

### 🚀 Essential Commands

This is the "skeleton" of the SDD process, responsible for the physical generation and phased delivery of artifacts.

1. **`/speckit.constitution`**1. Constitutionalism. Define project meta-rules (such as technology stack, coding standards, and quality gates).

2.`/speckit.specify`**Specification definition. Capturing signals of business intent (Focus on What).

3. **`/speckit.plan`**Technology Planning.** Constructing a "digital twin" of the technological logic (Focus on How).

4. **`/speckit.tasks`**Task decomposition.** Decouple the plan into atomic execution steps.

5. **`/speckit.implement`**: Implementation. Automatic coding based on the task sheet.

### 🛡️ Assisted Quality and Collaboration (Optional Commands)
This is the "nervous system" of SDD, ensuring that intent does not drift during the flow.

- **`/speckit.clarify`**Requirements Clarification.** **A separate entry point for human-computer collaboration.** Developers can run it at any time to...`specify`Asking follow-up questions and providing additional information does not necessarily depend on the checklist being triggered.

- **`/speckit.analyze`**: Consistency audit. In`/tasks`Then run it to scan for conflicts in the entire document chain.

- **`/speckit.checklist`**: Quality Checklist. As a "unit test for English language requirements," implement full-phase quality gatekeeping.

- **`/speckit.taskstoissues`**Collaborative Extension:** Primarily used for task synchronization (non-core paths) in GitHub scenarios.

---

### 🎨 Expert-Level Full-Phase Spiral Sequence Diagram

Understanding SDD hinges on understanding the **Workflow Loop**. The process is no longer unidirectional, but rather a spiraling upward pattern driven by **Checklist (Quality Gate)**.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    autonumber
    participant U as 👤 Human (User)
    participant A as 🧠 AI Agent (Spec-Kit)
    participant F as 📄 Artifacts (文档/产物)
    participant G as 🚀 GitHub (协同层)

    Note over U, F: [阶段 1: Genesis - 立宪]
    U->>A: /speckit.constitution (治理原则)
    A->>F: 写入规则与技术栈约束

    Note over U, F: [阶段 2: Definition - 意图定义]
    U->>A: /speckit.specify (业务规格)
    A->>F: 生成 Spec.md
    
    opt Checkpoint 1: 需求自检 (After Specify)
        U->>A: /speckit.checklist
        A->>F: 扫描 Spec 颗粒度
        A-->>U: C1: 提醒补全 User Story
    end

    rect rgb(240, 248, 255)
    Note right of U: 随时待命的交互入口
    U->>A: /speckit.clarify (人机对齐)
    A->>F: 消除歧义并自动回填 Spec.md
    end

    Note over U, F: [阶段 3: Design - 技术规划]
    U->>A: /speckit.plan (技术规划)
    A->>F: 生成 Plan.md
    
    opt Checkpoint 2: 反向需求校验 (After Plan)
        U->>A: /speckit.checklist
        A->>F: 交叉对比 Spec & Plan
        alt 发现技术与需求脱节 (Path A/B)
            A-->>U: C2: 警告：实现方案超出了需求定义范围
            U->>A: 回溯修改 Spec 或调用 Clarify
        end
    end

    Note over U, F: [阶段 4: Engineering - 任务拆解]
    U->>A: /speckit.tasks (任务解耦)
    A->>F: 生成 Tasks.md
    
    opt Checkpoint 3: 执行完整性校验 (After Tasks)
        U->>A: /speckit.checklist
        A->>F: 扫描 Tasks 是否覆盖所有 AC
        A-->>U: C3: 提醒遗漏的边界测试任务
    end

    Note over U, F: [阶段 5: Audit & Sync - 审计与同步]
    U->>A: /speckit.analyze (三方全产物审计)
    A->>F: 跨文档一致性扫描 (Passes 1-6)
    
    opt 协同扩展
        U->>A: /speckit.taskstoissues
        A->>G: 同步本地 Tasks 至 GitHub Issues
    end

    Note over U, F: [阶段 6: Execution - 意图落地]
    U->>A: /speckit.implement (自动化编码)
    
    critical Checkpoint 4: 门禁状态机判定
        A->>F: 触发最后扫描：是否有未勾选的 [ ] ?
        option 存在 [ ] 未完成
            A-->>U: 🚨 STOP: 必须处理余下的 Checklist 项
        option 全部 [x] 已完成
            A->>F: 开始编码 (TDD) 并自动回写进度
    end
```## 3. In-depth Analysis: Decision Logic of the Spiral Path A/B/C

As the **"Quality Gate"** of the entire system, the operation...`checklist`The final result will determine the physical trajectory of the project:

### 🔄 Spiral Path A: Backtrack Specification (Discovery → Clarify → Backtrack)

**Scenario**: AI in`plan`Technical signals (such as caching mechanisms) were extracted at each stage, but it was found that...`spec.md`The specific business failure strategy was omitted.

- **Decision: Backtrack to`specify`.use **`/speckit.clarify`**Used as an entry point for backtracking.

**Action:** AI asks a question through clarification interaction → User answers and the system automatically fills in the blanks.`spec.md`→ Rerun`/speckit.plan`Refresh the plan.

- **Philosophy**:`clarify`Not subordinate to`checklist`It's a ready-to-use dialogue window, used to keep the intent alive.

### 🔄 Spiral Path B: Refactoring Execution (Risk → Refine)

**Scenario**: AI in`tasks`The phase revealed that task descriptions contained weak signal words such as "approximately" or "possibly," or that the task granularity was too large to be independently verified.

- **Decision:** Stay at`tasks`The process involves restructuring at each stage.

- **Action:** AI re-decomposes atomic tasks based on the checklist until each task item meets the standard of "independent testing and dependency-free execution."

### 🔄 Spiral Path C: Quality Release (Validated → Proceed)

**Scenario:** The checklist shows that all acceptance criteria have been quantified, boundaries are clearly defined, and tasks are fully covered.

- **Action:** Proceed forward to **`/speckit.analyze`** Conduct third-party audits (Spec/Plan/Tasks consistency checks) → After confirming no errors, proceed to the actual AI automated coding stage.

---

> [!IMPORTANT]

> **GitHub Spec Kit Hardcore Constraints: Why`/implement`Will it "force a stop"?

> This is the most hardcore **State Machine** constraint in the Spec Kit.`implement`In the source code logic, AI will scan`checklists/`All Markdown files in the directory`[ ]`Status. If not all are checked.`[x]`Or, if AI determines that a critical path has not passed through Clarify, the underlying script will **forcefully lock** code generation. This forces every line of production code to have a corresponding "intent stub".

## 4. Best Practices: Expert Advice

### A. Acceptance Criteria Must Be "Measurable and Triggerable"

- ❌ **Incorrect Practice**: "Ensure the system is smooth and stable, and avoids errors."

- ✅ **Correct Practice**: "AC2: API QPS threshold must be greater than 200, and trigger proactive caching and traffic switching after a 1.5s timeout."

### B. Logic Diversion for Bug Handling

If an error occurs during code implementation, should the code be modified or the documentation modified?

1. **Execution Layer Failure**: If the code logic conforms to the specification but fails due to minor environmental or syntactic flaws, have the Agent directly provide a patch.

2. **Logic Omission**: **Strictly refuse to directly modify the source code**. Go back to the top level and first write the omission rules.`spec.md` or `plan.md`The generation process is re-flowed from top to bottom, ensuring a zero Spec Drift rate.

## What's Next: The Future Evolution of SDDs

**GitHub Spec Kit** provides a mature enterprise-grade intent console that truly realizes the leap from "code-centric" to "specification-centric" software engineering through a sophisticated workflow of 9 commands. This shift not only improves development efficiency but also lays the foundation for deep collaboration with AI Agents.

In the next article, [Part 3: Intent Layer Infrastructure and the Future of SDDs](/posts/sdd-series-part-1-evolution/), we will explore how SDDs will evolve into a new type of fully autonomous, iterative "autonomous control plane" as LLM's self-managed memory capabilities rapidly increase.
